12	from uuid import uuid4
13	
14	from pydantic import BaseModel
15	
16	from noosphere.models import (
17	    CascadeEdgeRelation,
18	    Method,
19	    MethodImplRef,
20	    MethodInvocation,
21	    MethodType,
22	)
23	from noosphere.methods._hooks import _FAILURE_HOOKS, _POST_HOOKS, _PRE_HOOKS
24	from noosphere.methods._registry import REGISTRY
25	from noosphere.methods.composition import validate_depends_on
26	
27	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
28	
29	CORRELATION_ID: contextvars.ContextVar[str] = contextvars.ContextVar(
30	    "CORRELATION_ID", default=""
31	)
32	TENANT_ID: contextvars.ContextVar[str] = contextvars.ContextVar(
33	    "TENANT_ID", default="default"
34	)
35	
36	_store_factory: Callable[..., Any] | None = None
37	
38	
39	def set_store_factory(factory: Callable[..., Any]) -> None:
40	    global _store_factory
41	    _store_factory = factory
42	
43	
44	def _get_store() -> Any:
45	    if _store_factory is not None:
46	        try:
47	            return _store_factory()
48	        except Exception:
49	            return None
50	    return None
51	
52	
53	def _canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:
54	    if isinstance(obj, BaseModel):
55	        obj = obj.model_dump(mode="json")
56	    return json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)
57	
58	
59	def _current_git_sha() -> str:
60	    sha = os.environ.get("THESEUS_GIT_SHA")
61	    if sha:
62	        return sha
63	    try:
64	        result = subprocess.run(
65	            ["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"],
66	            capture_output=True,
67	            text=True,
68	            timeout=5,
69	        )
70	        if result.returncode == 0:
71	            return result.stdout.strip()
72	    except Exception:
73	        pass
74	    return "unknown"
75	
76	
77	def _current_correlation_id() -> str:
78	    return CORRELATION_ID.get()
79	
80	
81	def _current_tenant_id() -> str:
82	    return TENANT_ID.get()
83	
84	
85	def _validate_against_schema(schema: Any, data: Any) -> Any:
86	    if isinstance(schema, type) and issubclass(schema, BaseModel):
87	        if isinstance(data, schema):
88	            return data
89	        return schema.model_validate(data)
90	    if isinstance(schema, dict) and schema:
91	        try:
92	            import jsonschema as _js
93	
94	            _js.validate(data, schema)
95	        except ImportError:
96	            pass
97	    return data
98	
99	
100	def _utcnow() -> datetime:
101	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
102	
103	
104	def _run_failure_path(
105	    spec: Method,
106	    inv: MethodInvocation,
107	    validated_input: Any,
108	    exc: BaseException,
109	) -> None:
110	    inv = inv.model_copy(
111	        update={
112	            "ended_at": _utcnow(),
113	            "succeeded": False,
114	            "error_kind": type(exc).__name__,
115	        }
116	    )
117	    for hook_name, hook in _FAILURE_HOOKS:
118	        try:
119	            hook(spec, inv, validated_input, exc)
120	        except Exception:
121	            logger.exception("Failure hook %s raised", hook_name)
122	    store = _get_store()
123	    if store is not None:
124	        try:
125	            store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
126	        except Exception:
127	            logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
128	
129	
130	def register_method(
131	    *,
132	    name: str,
133	    version: str,
134	    method_type: MethodType,
135	    input_schema: Any,
136	    output_schema: Any,
137	    description: str,
138	    rationale: str,
139	    preconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
140	    postconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
141	    dependencies: list[tuple[str, str]] | tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (),
142	    depends_on: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
143	    owner: str,
144	    status: str = "active",
145	    nondeterministic: bool = False,
146	    emits_edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation] | tuple[CascadeEdgeRelation, ...] = (),
147	) -> Callable:
148	    def decorator(fn: Callable) -> Callable:
149	        if isinstance(input_schema, type) and issubclass(input_schema, BaseModel):
150	            in_schema_dict = input_schema.model_json_schema()
151	        elif isinstance(input_schema, dict):
152	            in_schema_dict = input_schema
153	        else:
154	            in_schema_dict = {}
155	
156	        if isinstance(output_schema, type) and issubclass(output_schema, BaseModel):
157	            out_schema_dict = output_schema.model_json_schema()
158	        elif isinstance(output_schema, dict):
159	            out_schema_dict = output_schema
160	        else:
161	            out_schema_dict = {}
162	
163	        impl = MethodImplRef(
164	            module=fn.__module__,
165	            fn_name=fn.__qualname__,
166	            git_sha=_current_git_sha(),
167	            image_digest=None,
168	        )
169	
170	        spec_for_hash = {
171	            "name": name,
172	            "version": version,
173	            "method_type": method_type.value,
174	            "input_schema": in_schema_dict,
175	            "output_schema": out_schema_dict,
176	            "description": description,
177	            "rationale": rationale,
178	            "preconditions": list(preconditions),
179	            "postconditions": list(postconditions),
180	            "dependencies": [list(d) for d in dependencies],
181	            "implementation": impl.model_dump(mode="json"),
182	            "owner": owner,
183	            "status": status,
184	            "nondeterministic": nondeterministic,
185	        }
186	
187	        method_id = hashlib.sha256(
188	            _canonical_json(spec_for_hash).encode()
189	        ).hexdigest()[:32]
190	
191	        spec = Method(
192	            method_id=method_id,
193	            name=name,
194	            version=version,
195	            method_type=method_type,
196	            input_schema=in_schema_dict,
197	            output_schema=out_schema_dict,
198	            description=description,
199	            rationale=rationale,
200	            preconditions=list(preconditions),
201	            postconditions=list(postconditions),
202	            dependencies=list(dependencies),
203	            implementation=impl,
204	            owner=owner,
205	            status=status,
206	            nondeterministic=nondeterministic,
207	            created_at=_utcnow(),
208	        )
209	
210	        @wraps(fn)
211	        def wrapped(input_data: Any) -> Any:
212	            validated_input = _validate_against_schema(input_schema, input_data)
213	            input_hash = hashlib.sha256(
214	                _canonical_json(validated_input).encode()
215	            ).hexdigest()
216	
217	            inv = MethodInvocation(
218	                id=str(uuid4()),
219	                method_id=spec.method_id,
220	                input_hash=input_hash,
221	                output_hash="",
222	                started_at=_utcnow(),
223	                ended_at=None,
224	                succeeded=False,
225	                error_kind=None,
226	                correlation_id=_current_correlation_id(),
227	                tenant_id=_current_tenant_id(),
228	            )
229	
230	            try:
231	                for _hook_name, hook in _PRE_HOOKS:
232	                    hook(spec, inv, validated_input)
233	            except Exception as e:
234	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
235	                raise
236	
237	            try:
238	                result = fn(validated_input)
239	                _validate_against_schema(output_schema, result)
240	                output_hash = hashlib.sha256(
241	                    _canonical_json(result).encode()
242	                ).hexdigest()
243	                inv = inv.model_copy(
244	                    update={
245	                        "ended_at": _utcnow(),
246	                        "succeeded": True,
247	                        "output_hash": output_hash,
248	                    }
249	                )
250	
251	                for hook_name, hook in _POST_HOOKS:
252	                    try:
253	                        hook(spec, inv, validated_input, result)
254	                    except Exception:
255	                        logger.exception("Post-hook %s raised", hook_name)
256	
257	                store = _get_store()
258	                if store is not None:
259	                    try:
260	                        store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
261	                    except Exception:
262	                        logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
263	
264	                return result
265	            except Exception as e:
266	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
267	                raise
268	
269	        wrapped.__method_spec__ = spec  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
270	
271	        REGISTRY.register(spec, wrapped)
272	
273	        # Composition DAG: declared depends_on names must already be
274	        # registered. Decoration-time check so a typo fails CI rather
275	        # than silently leaving a ghost edge in the graph.
276	        if depends_on:
277	            validate_depends_on(
278	                REGISTRY.known_method_names(), name, list(depends_on)
279	            )
280	            REGISTRY.set_depends_on(name, list(depends_on))
281	        else:
282	            # Always seed the side table so build_dag() sees this node.
283	            REGISTRY.set_depends_on(name, [])
284	
285	        if emits_edges:
286	            REGISTRY.set_emits_edges(method_id, list(emits_edges))
287	
288	        store = _get_store()
289	        if store is not None:
290	            try:
291	                store.insert_method(spec)
292	            except Exception:
293	                logger.exception("Failed to persist method spec")
294	
295	        return wrapped
296	
297	    return decorator
298

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from typing import Callable
4	
5	from noosphere.models import CascadeEdgeRelation, Method
6	
7	
8	class MethodCollisionError(Exception):
9	    pass
10	
11	
12	class MethodNotFoundError(Exception):
13	    pass
14	
15	
16	def _parse_semver(version: str) -> tuple[int, ...]:
17	    parts: list[int] = []
18	    for p in version.split("."):
19	        try:
20	            parts.append(int(p))
21	        except ValueError:
22	            parts.append(0)
23	    return tuple(parts)
24	
25	
26	class MethodRegistry:
27	    def __init__(self) -> None:
28	        self._specs: dict[tuple[str, str], Method] = {}
29	        self._fns: dict[tuple[str, str], Callable] = {}
30	        self._emits_edges: dict[str, list[CascadeEdgeRelation]] = {}
31	        # name -> list of dependency method names (composition DAG).
32	        # Keyed on bare name because the DAG is about method identity,
33	        # not about a particular pinned version.
34	        self._depends_on: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
35	
36	    def register(self, spec: Method, fn: Callable) -> None:
37	        key = (spec.name, spec.version)
38	        if key in self._specs:
39	            raise MethodCollisionError(
40	                f"Method {spec.name} v{spec.version} already registered"
41	            )
42	        self._specs[key] = spec
43	        self._fns[key] = fn
44	
45	    def set_emits_edges(
46	        self, method_id: str, edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation]
47	    ) -> None:
48	        self._emits_edges[method_id] = list(edges)
49	
50	    def get_emits_edges(self, method_id: str) -> list[CascadeEdgeRelation]:
51	        return self._emits_edges.get(method_id, [])
52	
53	    # ── composition DAG side table ────────────────────────────────────
54	
55	    def set_depends_on(self, name: str, deps: list[str]) -> None:
56	        self._depends_on[name] = list(deps)
57	
58	    def get_depends_on(self, name: str) -> list[str]:
59	        return list(self._depends_on.get(name, []))
60	
61	    def iter_depends_on(self):
62	        for name, deps in self._depends_on.items():
63	            yield name, list(deps)
64	
65	    def known_method_names(self) -> set[str]:
66	        return {n for (n, _v) in self._specs.keys()}
67	
68	    def get(
69	        self,
70	        name: str,
71	        version: str = "latest",
72	        include_retired: bool = False,
73	    ) -> tuple[Method, Callable]:
74	        if version != "latest":
75	            key = (name, version)
76	            if key not in self._specs:
77	                raise MethodNotFoundError(
78	                    f"Method {name} v{version} not found"
79	                )
80	            spec = self._specs[key]
81	            if spec.status == "retired" and not include_retired:
82	                raise MethodNotFoundError(
83	                    f"Method {name} v{version} is retired"
84	                )
85	            return spec, self._fns[key]
86	
87	        candidates: list[tuple[str, Method]] = []
88	        for (n, v), spec in self._specs.items():
89	            if n != name:
90	                continue
91	            if spec.status == "retired" and not include_retired:
92	                continue
93	            candidates.append((v, spec))
94	
95	        if not candidates:
96	            raise MethodNotFoundError(f"No active method found for {name}")
97	
98	        candidates.sort(key=lambda x: _parse_semver(x[0]), reverse=True)
99	        best_version = candidates[0][0]
100	        return self._specs[(name, best_version)], self._fns[(name, best_version)]
101	
102	    def list(self, status_filter: str | None = None) -> list[Method]:
103	        specs = list(self._specs.values())
104	        if status_filter is not None:
105	            specs = [s for s in specs if s.status == status_filter]
106	        return specs
107	
108	
109	REGISTRY = MethodRegistry()
110

from noosphere.methods._decorator import (
    CORRELATION_ID,
    TENANT_ID,
    register_method,
    set_store_factory,
)
from noosphere.methods._hooks import (
    register_failure_hook,
    register_post_hook,
    register_pre_hook,
    unregister_hook,
)
from noosphere.methods._registry import (
    REGISTRY,
    MethodCollisionError,
    MethodNotFoundError,
)
from noosphere.methods.composition import (
    InheritedRisk,
    MethodCompositionError,
    MethodDag,
    MethodNode,
    build_dag,
    compute_risk_inheritance,
    graph_snapshot,
    severity_penalty_multiplier_with_inheritance,
)


def get_method(
    name: str, version: str = "latest", include_retired: bool = False
):
    return REGISTRY.get(name, version=version, include_retired=include_retired)


__all__ = [
    "CORRELATION_ID",
    "InheritedRisk",
    "MethodCollisionError",
    "MethodCompositionError",
    "MethodDag",
    "MethodNode",
    "MethodNotFoundError",
    "REGISTRY",
    "TENANT_ID",
    "build_dag",
    "compute_risk_inheritance",
    "get_method",
    "graph_snapshot",
    "register_failure_hook",
    "register_method",
    "register_post_hook",
    "register_pre_hook",
    "set_store_factory",
    "severity_penalty_multiplier_with_inheritance",
    "unregister_hook",
]

1	"""
2	Injectable embedding clients with on-disk cache keyed by (model, text hash).
3	"""
4	
5	from __future__ import annotations
6	
7	import hashlib
8	import json
9	import random
10	from pathlib import Path
11	from typing import Any, Protocol, runtime_checkable
12	
13	try:
14	    import numpy as np
15	except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - fallback is for broken local wheels.
16	    np = None  # type: ignore[assignment]
17	
18	
19	def _text_hash(model: str, text: str) -> str:
20	    h = hashlib.sha256()
21	    h.update(model.encode("utf-8"))
22	    h.update(b"\x00")
23	    h.update(text.encode("utf-8"))
24	    return h.hexdigest()
25	
26	
27	@runtime_checkable
28	class EmbeddingClient(Protocol):
29	    def encode(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]: ...
30	
31	    @property
32	    def model_name(self) -> str: ...
33	
34	
35	class DiskEmbeddingCache:
36	    """On-disk cache: one JSON file per (model, exact text)."""
37	
38	    def __init__(self, root: Path) -> None:
39	        self.root = root
40	        self.root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
41	
42	    def path(self, model: str, text: str) -> Path:
43	        return self.root / f"{_text_hash(model, text)}.json"
44	
45	    def get(self, model: str, text: str) -> list[float] | None:
46	        p = self.path(model, text)
47	        if not p.is_file():
48	            return None
49	        data = json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
50	        vec = data.get("embedding")
51	        if isinstance(vec, list):
52	            return [float(x) for x in vec]
53	        return None
54	
55	    def put(self, model: str, text: str, vector: list[float]) -> None:
56	        p = self.path(model, text)
57	        p.write_text(
58	            json.dumps({"model": model, "embedding": vector}, separators=(",", ":")),
59	            encoding="utf-8",
60	        )
61	
62	
63	class SentenceTransformersClient:
64	    """Local sentence-transformers backend."""
65	
66	    def __init__(
67	        self,
68	        model_name: str,
69	        *,
70	        device: str | None = None,
71	        cache_dir: Path | None = None,
72	    ) -> None:
73	        from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
74	
75	        self._model_name = model_name
76	        self._cache = DiskEmbeddingCache(cache_dir) if cache_dir is not None else None
77	        kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {}
78	        if device:
79	            kwargs["device"] = device
80	        self._model = SentenceTransformer(model_name, **kwargs)
81	
82	    @property
83	    def model_name(self) -> str:
84	        return self._model_name
85	
86	    def encode(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
87	        out: list[list[float]] = []
88	        for t in texts:
89	            if self._cache is not None:
90	                hit = self._cache.get(self._model_name, t)
91	                if hit is not None:
92	                    out.append(hit)
93	                    continue
94	            vec_np = self._model.encode(
95	                [t],
96	                convert_to_numpy=np is not None,
97	                show_progress_bar=False,
98	            )
99	            if np is not None:
100	                vec = np.asarray(vec_np[0], dtype=float).tolist()
101	            else:
102	                vec = [float(x) for x in vec_np[0]]
103	            if self._cache is not None:
104	                self._cache.put(self._model_name, t, vec)
105	            out.append(vec)
106	        return out
107	
108	
109	class MockEmbeddingClient:
110	    """Deterministic fake vectors for tests (no model download)."""
111	
112	    def __init__(self, model_name: str = "mock-embed", dim: int = 8) -> None:
113	        self._model_name = model_name
114	        self._dim = dim
115	
116	    @property
117	    def model_name(self) -> str:
118	        return self._model_name
119	
120	    def encode(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
121	        out: list[list[float]] = []
122	        for t in texts:
123	            seed = int(hashlib.sha256(t.encode()).hexdigest()[:8], 16)
124	            if np is not None:
125	                rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
126	                out.append(rng.standard_normal(self._dim).astype(float).tolist())
127	            else:
128	                rng = random.Random(seed)
129	                out.append([rng.gauss(0.0, 1.0) for _ in range(self._dim)])
130	        return out
131	
132	
133	def default_cache_dir() -> Path:
134	    from noosphere.config import get_settings
135	
136	    d = get_settings().data_dir / "embedding_cache"
137	    d.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
138	    return d
139	
140	
141	def sentence_transformers_client_from_settings() -> SentenceTransformersClient:
142	    from noosphere.config import get_settings
143	
144	    s = get_settings()
145	    return SentenceTransformersClient(
146	        s.embedding_model_name,
147	        device=s.embedding_device or None,
148	        cache_dir=default_cache_dir(),
149	    )
150

1	"""CLI commands for the Method Registry subsystem."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import json
6	from datetime import datetime, timezone
7	from typing import Optional
8	
9	import click
10	from rich.console import Console
11	from rich.table import Table
12	
13	console = Console()
14	
15	
16	def _get_store():
17	    from noosphere.cli import get_orchestrator
18	    return get_orchestrator(None).store
19	
20	
21	def _render(obj, as_json: bool) -> None:
22	    if as_json:
23	        click.echo(json.dumps(obj, indent=2, default=str))
24	
25	
26	@click.group("methods")
27	def cli() -> None:
28	    """Method registry: list, inspect, run, and diff registered methods."""
29	
30	
31	@cli.command("list")
32	@click.option("--status", "status_filter", type=str, default=None,
33	              help="Filter by status (experimental, active, deprecated, retired)")
34	@click.option("--json", "as_json", is_flag=True, help="Output as JSON")
35	def list_methods(status_filter: Optional[str], as_json: bool) -> None:
36	    """List all registered methods."""
37	    from noosphere.methods import REGISTRY
38	
39	    methods = REGISTRY.list(status_filter=status_filter)
40	    if as_json:
41	        _render([{"name": m.name, "version": m.version, "status": m.status,
42	                  "type": m.method_type.value, "description": m.description}
43	                 for m in methods], True)
44	        return
45	    if not methods:
46	        console.print("[yellow]No methods registered.[/yellow]")
47	        return
48	    table = Table(title="Registered Methods", show_header=True)
49	    table.add_column("Name", style="cyan")
50	    table.add_column("Version")
51	    table.add_column("Status")
52	    table.add_column("Type")
53	    table.add_column("Description", max_width=50)
54	    for m in methods:
55	        table.add_row(m.name, m.version, m.status, m.method_type.value,
56	                      m.description[:50])
57	    console.print(table)

102	@click.argument("ref_b")
103	@click.option("--json", "as_json", is_flag=True, help="Output as JSON")
104	def diff_methods(ref_a: str, ref_b: str, as_json: bool) -> None:
105	    """Diff two method versions. Each REF is name@version."""
106	    from noosphere.methods import REGISTRY
107	
108	    name_a, ver_a = (ref_a.split("@", 1) + ["latest"])[:2]
109	    name_b, ver_b = (ref_b.split("@", 1) + ["latest"])[:2]
110	    m_a, _ = REGISTRY.get(name_a, version=ver_a)
111	    m_b, _ = REGISTRY.get(name_b, version=ver_b)
112	    d_a, d_b = m_a.model_dump(), m_b.model_dump()
113	    diffs = {k: {"old": d_a[k], "new": d_b[k]}
114	             for k in d_a if d_a.get(k) != d_b.get(k)}
115	    if as_json:
116	        _render(diffs, True)
117	        return
118	    if not diffs:
119	        console.print("[green]Methods are identical.[/green]")
120	        return
121	    table = Table(title=f"Diff: {ref_a} ↔ {ref_b}", show_header=True)
122	    table.add_column("Field", style="cyan")
123	    table.add_column("Old")
124	    table.add_column("New")
125	    for field, vals in diffs.items():
126	        table.add_row(field, str(vals["old"])[:60], str(vals["new"])[:60])
127	    console.print(table)

196	            row["method"],
197	            str(row["modes"]),
198	            str(row["high_severity"]),

230	def _open_codex():
231	    from noosphere.codex_bridge import (
232	        REAL_DICT_CURSOR,
233	        _open_codex_connection,
234	        _resolve_codex_db_url,
235	    )

238	    conn = _open_codex_connection(url)
239	    return conn, REAL_DICT_CURSOR

242	def _resolve_org_id(cur, organization_slug: Optional[str]) -> Optional[str]:
243	    if not organization_slug:
244	        return None
245	    cur.execute(
246	        'SELECT id FROM "Organization" WHERE slug = %s',
247	        (organization_slug,),
248	    )
249	    row = cur.fetchone()
250	    if not row:
251	        raise click.ClickException(
252	            f"Organization slug not found: {organization_slug}"
253	        )
254	    return row["id"] if isinstance(row, dict) else row[0]
255	
256	
257	def _conclusions_with_profiles(cur, *, organization_id: Optional[str], limit: int):
258	    """Conclusions that have at least one MethodologyProfile attached, either
259	    directly or via the upload bridge."""
260	    cur.execute(
261	        '''SELECT DISTINCT c.id AS id,
262	                  c."organizationId" AS org_id,
263	                  c.text             AS text,
264	                  c."topicHint"      AS topic_hint
265	             FROM "Conclusion" c
266	             JOIN "MethodologyProfile" mp
267	                  ON mp."conclusionId" = c.id
268	                  OR mp."uploadId" IN (
269	                       SELECT cs."uploadId" FROM "ConclusionSource" cs
270	                        WHERE cs."conclusionId" = c.id
271	                  )
272	            WHERE (%s IS NULL OR c."organizationId" = %s)
273	            ORDER BY c.id
274	            LIMIT %s''',
275	        (organization_id, organization_id, int(limit)),
276	    )
277	    return list(cur.fetchall())
278	
279	
280	def _profiles_for(cur, *, organization_id: str, conclusion_id: str):
281	    from noosphere.evaluation.mqs import MethodologyProfileSummary
282	
283	    cur.execute(
284	        '''SELECT mp."patternType", mp.title, mp.summary,
285	                  mp."reasoningMoves", mp."transferTargets",
286	                  mp.assumptions, mp."failureModes", mp.confidence
287	             FROM "MethodologyProfile" mp
288	             LEFT JOIN "ConclusionSource" cs ON cs."uploadId" = mp."uploadId"
289	            WHERE mp."organizationId" = %s
290	              AND (mp."conclusionId" = %s OR cs."conclusionId" = %s)
291	            ORDER BY mp.confidence DESC
292	            LIMIT 8''',
293	        (organization_id, conclusion_id, conclusion_id),
294	    )
295	
296	    def _aslist(raw):
297	        if raw is None:
298	            return []
299	        if isinstance(raw, list):
300	            return [str(x) for x in raw if x is not None]
301	        if isinstance(raw, str):
302	            try:
303	                v = json.loads(raw)
304	                return [str(x) for x in v if x is not None] if isinstance(v, list) else []
305	            except Exception:
306	                return []
307	        return []
308	
309	    out = []
310	    for row in cur.fetchall():
311	        out.append(
312	            MethodologyProfileSummary(
313	                pattern_type=(row.get("patternType") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[0]) or "",
314	                title=(row.get("title") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[1]) or "",
315	                summary=(row.get("summary") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[2]) or "",
316	                reasoning_moves=_aslist(
317	                    row.get("reasoningMoves") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[3]
318	                ),
319	                transfer_targets=_aslist(
320	                    row.get("transferTargets") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[4]
321	                ),
322	                assumptions=_aslist(
323	                    row.get("assumptions") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[5]
324	                ),
325	                failure_modes=_aslist(
326	                    row.get("failureModes") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[6]
327	                ),
328	                confidence=float(
329	                    (row.get("confidence") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[7]) or 0.5
330	                ),
331	            )
332	        )
333	    return out
334

336	@cli.command("track-record")
337	@click.option(
338	    "--organization-slug",
339	    type=str,
340	    default=None,
341	    help="Restrict to a single organization (default: all orgs).",
342	)
343	@click.option(
344	    "--rebuild/--no-rebuild",
345	    default=False,
346	    help="Run the linker + aggregator pipeline before printing. Idempotent.",
347	)
348	@click.option(
349	    "--judge",
350	    "judge_name",
351	    type=click.Choice(["stub"]),
352	    default="stub",
353	    help="Linker judge to use. Only the deterministic stub is exposed via "
354	    "the CLI; production callers may construct their own MethodLinkerJudge.",
355	)
356	@click.option(
357	    "--limit",
358	    type=int,
359	    default=500,
360	    help="Maximum conclusions to (re)link in this run.",
361	)
362	@click.option("--json", "as_json", is_flag=True, help="Output as JSON")
363	def track_record(
364	    organization_slug: Optional[str],
365	    rebuild: bool,
366	    judge_name: str,
367	    limit: int,
368	    as_json: bool,
369	) -> None:
370	    """Show per-method track record. With --rebuild, first re-link every
371	    conclusion to its methods and re-aggregate every (method, domain) cell.
372	    Both passes are idempotent: re-running on a quiet DB is a no-op modulo
373	    `updatedAt`."""
374	    from noosphere.evaluation.method_outcome_linker import (
375	        StubMethodLinkerJudge,
376	        infer_links,
377	        registry_view,
378	        upsert_links,
379	    )
380	    from noosphere.evaluation.method_track_record import (
381	        aggregate,
382	        fetch_method_keys,
383	        fetch_resolved_rows,
384	        upsert_track_record,
385	    )
386	
387	    conn, real_dict_cursor = _open_codex()
388	    try:
389	        cur = conn.cursor(cursor_factory=real_dict_cursor)
390	        organization_id = _resolve_org_id(cur, organization_slug)
391	
392	        if rebuild:
393	            registry = registry_view()
394	            judge = StubMethodLinkerJudge()
395	            now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
396	
397	            conclusions = _conclusions_with_profiles(
398	                cur, organization_id=organization_id, limit=limit
399	            )
400	            linked = 0
401	            for c in conclusions:
402	                cid = c["id"] if isinstance(c, dict) else c[0]
403	                org = c["org_id"] if isinstance(c, dict) else c[1]
404	                text = (c.get("text") if isinstance(c, dict) else c[2]) or ""
405	                topic = (c.get("topic_hint") if isinstance(c, dict) else c[3]) or ""
406	                profiles = _profiles_for(cur, organization_id=org, conclusion_id=cid)
407	                if not profiles:
408	                    continue
409	                links = infer_links(
410	                    conclusion_id=cid,
411	                    conclusion_text=text,
412	                    topic_hint=topic,
413	                    profiles=profiles,
414	                    registry_methods=registry,
415	                    judge=judge,
416	                )
417	                if not links:
418	                    continue
419	                linked += upsert_links(
420	                    cur,
421	                    organization_id=org,
422	                    conclusion_id=cid,
423	                    links=links,
424	                    now=now,
425	                )
426	
427	            # Aggregate per (org, method, version). Walk every distinct
428	            # method seen in ConclusionMethod so we don't miss methods
429	            # that no longer match a registered name.
430	            cur.execute(
431	                'SELECT id FROM "Organization"'
432	                + (' WHERE id = %s' if organization_id else ''),
433	                (organization_id,) if organization_id else (),
434	            )
435	            org_ids = [
436	                (row.get("id") if isinstance(row, dict) else row[0])
437	                for row in cur.fetchall()
438	            ]
439	            aggregated = 0
440	            for org_id in org_ids:
441	                for (m_name, m_version) in fetch_method_keys(
442	                    cur, organization_id=org_id
443	                ):
444	                    rows = fetch_resolved_rows(
445	                        cur,
446	                        organization_id=org_id,
447	                        method_name=m_name,
448	                        method_version=m_version,
449	                    )
450	                    records = aggregate(
451	                        organization_id=org_id,
452	                        method_name=m_name,
453	                        method_version=m_version,
454	                        rows=rows,
455	                        now=now,
456	                    )
457	                    # Even when rows is empty, emit a sample_size=0 row
458	                    # for every (method, domain="") so downstream UI sees
459	                    # the method exists but has no record yet.
460	                    if not records:
461	                        records = aggregate(
462	                            organization_id=org_id,
463	                            method_name=m_name,
464	                            method_version=m_version,
465	                            rows=[],
466	                            now=now,
467	                        )
468	                        if not records:
469	                            from noosphere.evaluation.method_track_record import (
470	                                TrackRecord,
471	                                TRACK_RECORD_SCHEMA,
472	                            )
473	
474	                            records = [
475	                                TrackRecord(
476	                                    organization_id=org_id,
477	                                    method_name=m_name,
478	                                    method_version=m_version,
479	                                    domain="",
480	                                    sample_size=0,
481	                                    weighted_brier=None,
482	                                    calibration_slope=None,
483	                                    calibration_slope_ci_low=None,
484	                                    calibration_slope_ci_high=None,
485	                                    severity_pass_rate=None,
486	                                    evidence={
487	                                        "schema": TRACK_RECORD_SCHEMA,
488	                                        "prediction_ids": [],
489	                                        "conclusion_ids": [],
490	                                    },
491	                                    computed_at=now,
492	                                )
493	                            ]
494	                    for record in records:
495	                        upsert_track_record(cur, record)
496	                        aggregated += 1
497	            conn.commit()
498	            click.echo(
499	                json.dumps(
500	                    {"linked": linked, "aggregated": aggregated, "judge": judge_name},
501	                    indent=2,
502	                )
503	            )
504	
505	        # Print whatever is in MethodTrackRecord (post-rebuild or cached).
506	        cur.execute(
507	            '''SELECT "organizationId", "methodName", "methodVersion", domain,
508	                      "sampleSize", "weightedBrier",
509	                      "calibrationSlope", "calibrationSlopeCiLow",
510	                      "calibrationSlopeCiHigh", "severityPassRate",
511	                      "computedAt"
512	                 FROM "MethodTrackRecord"
513	                WHERE (%s IS NULL OR "organizationId" = %s)
514	                ORDER BY "methodName", "methodVersion", domain''',
515	            (organization_id, organization_id),
516	        )
517	        rows = list(cur.fetchall())
518	
519	        if as_json:
520	            click.echo(
521	                json.dumps(
522	                    [
523	                        {
524	                            "organization_id": r["organizationId"]
525	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[0],
526	                            "method_name": r["methodName"]
527	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[1],
528	                            "method_version": r["methodVersion"]
529	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[2],
530	                            "domain": r["domain"] if isinstance(r, dict) else r[3],
531	                            "sample_size": r["sampleSize"]
532	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[4],
533	                            "weighted_brier": r["weightedBrier"]
534	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[5],
535	                            "calibration_slope": r["calibrationSlope"]
536	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[6],
537	                            "calibration_slope_ci_low": r["calibrationSlopeCiLow"]
538	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[7],
539	                            "calibration_slope_ci_high": r["calibrationSlopeCiHigh"]
540	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[8],
541	                            "severity_pass_rate": r["severityPassRate"]
542	                            if isinstance(r, dict) else r[9],
543	                            "computed_at": str(
544	                                r["computedAt"] if isinstance(r, dict) else r[10]
545	                            ),
546	                        }
547	                        for r in rows
548	                    ],
549	                    indent=2,
550	                    default=str,
551	                )
552	            )
553	            return
554	
555	        if not rows:
556	            console.print(
557	                "[yellow]No track records yet. Run with --rebuild.[/yellow]"
558	            )
559	            return
560	
561	        table = Table(title="Method Track Records", show_header=True)
562	        table.add_column("Method", style="cyan")
563	        table.add_column("v")
564	        table.add_column("Domain")
565	        table.add_column("n")
566	        table.add_column("Brier")
567	        table.add_column("Slope")
568	        table.add_column("CI")
569	        table.add_column("Sev pass")
570	        for r in rows:
571	            def _g(key, idx):
572	                return r[key] if isinstance(r, dict) else r[idx]
573	
574	            slope = _g("calibrationSlope", 6)
575	            lo = _g("calibrationSlopeCiLow", 7)
576	            hi = _g("calibrationSlopeCiHigh", 8)
577	            ci = (
578	                f"[{lo:.2f}, {hi:.2f}]"
579	                if (lo is not None and hi is not None)
580	                else "—"
581	            )
582	            brier = _g("weightedBrier", 5)
583	            sev = _g("severityPassRate", 9)
584	            table.add_row(
585	                str(_g("methodName", 1)),
586	                str(_g("methodVersion", 2)),
587	                str(_g("domain", 3) or "—"),
588	                str(_g("sampleSize", 4)),
589	                f"{brier:.3f}" if brier is not None else "—",
590	                f"{slope:.2f}" if slope is not None else "—",
591	                ci,
592	                f"{sev:.0%}" if sev is not None else "—",
593	            )
594	        console.print(table)
595	    except Exception:
596	        conn.rollback()
597	        raise
598	    finally:
599	        conn.close()

"""Tests for the method registry."""
from __future__ import annotations

import os
from datetime import datetime, timezone

import pytest

from noosphere.models import Method, MethodImplRef, MethodType
from noosphere.methods._registry import (
    REGISTRY,
    MethodCollisionError,
    MethodNotFoundError,
    MethodRegistry,
)
from noosphere.methods._decorator import _canonical_json, register_method
from noosphere.methods._hooks import (
    _POST_HOOKS,
    _PRE_HOOKS,
    _FAILURE_HOOKS,
    unregister_hook,
)


def _make_impl() -> MethodImplRef:
    return MethodImplRef(
        module="test", fn_name="test_fn", git_sha="abc123", image_digest=None
    )


def _make_method(
    name: str, version: str, status: str = "active"
) -> Method:
    return Method(
        method_id=f"{name}_{version}_{status}",
        name=name,
        version=version,
        method_type=MethodType.EXTRACTION,
        input_schema={},
        output_schema={},
        description="test",
        rationale="test",
        preconditions=[],
        postconditions=[],
        dependencies=[],
        implementation=_make_impl(),
        owner="test",
        status=status,
        nondeterministic=False,
        created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
    )


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def _fresh_registry():
    """Give each test a clean registry."""
    reg = MethodRegistry()
    yield reg


class TestMethodRegistry:
    def test_register_two_versions(self):
        reg = MethodRegistry()
        m1 = _make_method("extractor", "1.0.0")
        m2 = _make_method("extractor", "2.0.0")
        reg.register(m1, lambda x: x)
        reg.register(m2, lambda x: x)
        assert len(reg.list()) == 2

    def test_get_latest_returns_highest_semver(self):
        reg = MethodRegistry()
        m1 = _make_method("extractor", "1.0.0")
        m2 = _make_method("extractor", "2.0.0")
        m3 = _make_method("extractor", "1.5.0")
        reg.register(m1, lambda x: x)
        reg.register(m2, lambda x: x)
        reg.register(m3, lambda x: x)
        spec, _fn = reg.get("extractor", "latest")
        assert spec.version == "2.0.0"

    def test_retired_not_returned_by_default(self):
        reg = MethodRegistry()
        m_active = _make_method("judge", "1.0.0", status="active")
        m_retired = _make_method("judge", "2.0.0", status="retired")
        reg.register(m_active, lambda x: x)
        reg.register(m_retired, lambda x: x)
        spec, _ = reg.get("judge", "latest")
        assert spec.version == "1.0.0"

    def test_retired_returned_with_flag(self):
        reg = MethodRegistry()
        m_retired = _make_method("judge", "2.0.0", status="retired")
        reg.register(m_retired, lambda x: x)
        spec, _ = reg.get("judge", "latest", include_retired=True)
        assert spec.version == "2.0.0"

    def test_collision_error(self):
        reg = MethodRegistry()
        m1 = _make_method("dup", "1.0.0")
        reg.register(m1, lambda x: x)
        m2 = _make_method("dup", "1.0.0")
        with pytest.raises(MethodCollisionError):
            reg.register(m2, lambda x: x)

    def test_not_found_error(self):
        reg = MethodRegistry()
        with pytest.raises(MethodNotFoundError):
            reg.get("unknown")

    def test_list_with_status_filter(self):
        reg = MethodRegistry()
        reg.register(_make_method("a", "1.0.0", "active"), lambda x: x)
        reg.register(_make_method("b", "1.0.0", "deprecated"), lambda x: x)
        active = reg.list(status_filter="active")
        assert len(active) == 1
        assert active[0].name == "a"

    def test_canonical_input_hashing(self):
        """Two calls with equal payloads produce equal input_hash."""
        payload_a = {"key": "value", "nested": {"b": 2, "a": 1}}
        payload_b = {"nested": {"a": 1, "b": 2}, "key": "value"}
        hash_a = _canonical_json(payload_a)
        hash_b = _canonical_json(payload_b)
        assert hash_a == hash_b

    def test_emits_edges_side_table(self):
        from noosphere.models import CascadeEdgeRelation

        reg = MethodRegistry()
        edges = [CascadeEdgeRelation.EXTRACTED_FROM]
        reg.set_emits_edges("m1", edges)
        assert reg.get_emits_edges("m1") == edges
        assert reg.get_emits_edges("nonexistent") == []

file.content:
from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
import logging
import os
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Optional

from pydantic import BaseModel

from noosphere.models import Actor, ContextMeta, LedgerEntry
from noosphere.ledger.keys import KeyRing

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def _canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:
    if isinstance(obj, BaseModel):
        obj = obj.model_dump(mode="json")
    return json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)


def _now_isoformat() -> str:
    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()


class Ledger:
    """Append-only signed, Merkle-chained audit log."""

    def __init__(self, store: Any, keyring: Optional[KeyRing] = None) -> None:
        self._store = store
        self._keyring = keyring

    @property
    def store(self) -> Any:
        return self._store

    @property
    def keyring(self) -> KeyRing:
        if self._keyring is None:
            raise RuntimeError("KeyRing not configured")
        return self._keyring

    def append(
        self,
        *,
        actor: Actor,
        method_id: str | None,
        inputs_hash: str,
        outputs_hash: str,
        inputs_ref: str,
        outputs_ref: str,
        context: ContextMeta,
    ) -> str:
        prev = self._store.ledger_tail()
        prev_hash = prev.entry_id if prev else ""
        payload = _canonical_json({
            "prev_hash": prev_hash,
            "timestamp": _now_isoformat(),
            "actor": actor.model_dump(mode="json"),
            "method_id": method_id,
            "inputs_hash": inputs_hash,
            "outputs_hash": outputs_hash,
            "inputs_ref": inputs_ref,
            "outputs_ref": outputs_ref,
            "context": context.model_dump(mode="json"),
        })
        entry_id = hashlib.sha256(
            prev_hash.encode() + payload.encode()
        ).hexdigest()
        signature_bytes = self.keyring.sign(entry_id.encode())
        entry = LedgerEntry(
            entry_id=entry_id,
            prev_hash=prev_hash,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            actor=actor,
            method_id=method_id,
            inputs_hash=inputs_hash,
            outputs_hash=outputs_hash,
            inputs_ref=inputs_ref,
            outputs_ref=outputs_ref,
            context=context,
            signature=signature_bytes.hex(),
            signer_key_id=self.keyring.active_key_id,
        )
        self._store.append_ledger_entry(entry)
        self._mirror_to_jsonl(entry)
        return entry_id

    def _mirror_to_jsonl(self, entry: LedgerEntry) -> None:
        mirror_dir = Path(
            os.environ.get("THESEUS_LEDGER_MIRROR_DIR", "~/.theseus/ledger")
        ).expanduser()
        try:
            mirror_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            filename = entry.timestamp.strftime("%Y-%m") + ".jsonl"
            filepath = mirror_dir / filename
            with open(filepath, "a") as f:
                f.write(entry.model_dump_json() + "\n")
        except Exception:
            logger.warning("Failed to mirror ledger entry to JSONL", exc_info=True)

1	"""Methodology Quality Score (MQS): operational form of the five working
2	criteria from THE_META_METHOD.md.
3	
4	Each Conclusion that has at least one MethodologyProfile gets an MQS row with
5	five sub-scores in [0,1], an LLM-or-stub-judged evidence blob per sub-score,
6	and a composite that uses Domain Sensitivity as a multiplicative gate.
7	
8	The composite formula and sub-score rubrics are documented in
9	docs/methods/MQS_Specification.md. The doc and this module are kept in sync
10	by scripts/check_mqs_doc_consistency.py.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import json
16	import re
17	from dataclasses import dataclass
18	from datetime import datetime, timezone
19	from typing import Any, Iterable, Optional, Protocol
20	
21	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
22	
23	
24	MQS_SCHEMA = "theseus.mqs.v1"
25	PROMPT_VERSION = "mqs-prompt-v1.0"
26	
27	# Mirrored verbatim by docs/methods/MQS_Specification.md and verified by
28	# scripts/check_mqs_doc_consistency.py.
29	COMPOSITE_FORMULA = (
30	    "domain_sensitivity * mean(progressivity, severity, aim_method_fit, compressibility)"
31	)
32	
33	SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS: dict[str, float] = {
34	    "progressivity": 0.25,
35	    "severity": 0.25,
36	    "aim_method_fit": 0.25,
37	    "compressibility": 0.25,
38	}
39	
40	_DECISION_RULE_RE = re.compile(
41	    r"\b(if [a-z]+.*?then |we will |we'll |exit if |by [0-9]{4}|by q[1-4] |"
42	    r"check back |trigger |would falsif|will revisit)",
43	    re.IGNORECASE,
44	)
45	_EVIDENCE_STR_CAP = 600
46	
47	
48	class MqsJudge(Protocol):
49	    """LLM-judge contract. Tests pass a stub; production wires the real LLM."""
50	
51	    def judge(self, *, criterion: str, prompt: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
52	        """Return a dict with at least {"score": float in [0,1], "rationale": str}."""
53	        ...
54	
55	
56	@dataclass
57	class StubMqsJudge:
58	    """Deterministic judge used by tests and by the no-LLM backfill path.
59	
60	    `responses` lets a test pin a per-criterion score; otherwise every call
61	    returns `default_score` with rationale "stub".
62	    """
63	
64	    responses: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] | None = None
65	    default_score: float = 0.5
66	
67	    def judge(self, *, criterion: str, prompt: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
68	        if self.responses and criterion in self.responses:
69	            return self.responses[criterion]
70	        return {"score": self.default_score, "rationale": "stub"}
71	
72	
73	class MethodologyProfileSummary(BaseModel):
74	    """Slimmed-down view of MethodologyProfile fields the scorer reads."""
75	
76	    pattern_type: str = ""
77	    title: str = ""
78	    summary: str = ""
79	    reasoning_moves: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
80	    transfer_targets: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
81	    assumptions: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
82	    failure_modes: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
83	    confidence: float = 0.5
84	
85	
86	class MqsInput(BaseModel):
87	    """Everything the scorer needs about a conclusion to produce an MQS."""
88	
89	    conclusion_id: str
90	    conclusion_text: str = ""
91	    rationale: str = ""
92	    topic_hint: str = ""
93	    profiles: list[MethodologyProfileSummary] = Field(default_factory=list)
94	    forecast_count: int = 0
95	    has_check_back_date: bool = False
96	    dissent_claim_count: int = 0
97	
98	    # Track-record coupling (prompt 02). Caller looks up the relevant
99	    # `MethodTrackRecord` row(s) for this conclusion's domain via
100	    # `noosphere.evaluation.method_track_record.severity_ceiling_for` and
101	    # passes the resulting cap here. None means "no track record available
102	    # / no cap"; a value caps `score_severity` from above so that methods
103	    # with thin or poor track records cannot project high severity onto
104	    # new conclusions in their domain.
105	    severity_track_record_ceiling: Optional[float] = None
106	
107	    # Drift coupling (prompt 04 + composition prompt). Multiplicative
108	    # penalty in (0, 1] applied to the Severity sub-score AFTER the
109	    # track-record ceiling. Callers derive this from the *inherited*
110	    # drift state across the method's composition DAG via
111	    # `noosphere.methods.composition.severity_penalty_multiplier_with_inheritance`,
112	    # NOT from the leaf state alone — a method whose composed dependency
113	    # is drifting must inherit that risk. 1.0 means "no penalty"
114	    # (default). Documented in docs/methods/MQS_Specification.md §
115	    # Drift coupling.
116	    severity_drift_penalty: float = 1.0
117	
118	
119	class SubScore(BaseModel):
120	    score: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
121	    evidence: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)
122	
123	    @field_validator("evidence")
124	    @classmethod
125	    def _cap_strings(cls, value: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
126	        return _cap_evidence(value)
127	
128	
129	class MethodologyQualityScore(BaseModel):
130	    """Operational form of the five meta-method criteria for one conclusion."""
131	
132	    schema_: str = Field(default=MQS_SCHEMA, alias="schema")
133	    conclusion_id: str
134	    progressivity: SubScore
135	    severity: SubScore
136	    aim_method_fit: SubScore
137	    compressibility: SubScore
138	    domain_sensitivity: SubScore
139	    composite: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
140	    composite_formula: str = COMPOSITE_FORMULA
141	    model_name: str = "stub"
142	    prompt_version: str = PROMPT_VERSION
143	    scored_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
144	
145	    model_config = {"populate_by_name": True}
146	
147	
148	def _cap_evidence(value: Any) -> Any:
149	    if isinstance(value, str):
150	        return value[:_EVIDENCE_STR_CAP]
151	    if isinstance(value, list):
152	        return [_cap_evidence(item) for item in value]
153	    if isinstance(value, dict):
154	        return {k: _cap_evidence(v) for k, v in value.items()}
155	    return value
156	
157	
158	def _clamp01(x: float) -> float:
159	    if x != x:  # NaN
160	        return 0.0
161	    return max(0.0, min(1.0, float(x)))
162	
163	
164	def _decision_rule_phrase_count(text: str) -> int:
165	    return len(_DECISION_RULE_RE.findall(text or ""))
166	
167	
168	def _aggregate_profile_lists(
169	    profiles: Iterable[MethodologyProfileSummary],
170	    field: str,
171	) -> list[str]:
172	    out: list[str] = []
173	    for p in profiles:
174	        out.extend(getattr(p, field, []) or [])
175	    return out
176	
177	
178	def score_progressivity(input_data: MqsInput) -> SubScore:
179	    """Deterministic. Reads forecast count, check-back, decision-rule phrases."""
180	    text = f"{input_data.conclusion_text} {input_data.rationale}"
181	    rule_phrases = _decision_rule_phrase_count(text)
182	    forecasts = max(0, int(input_data.forecast_count))
183	    has_check_back = bool(input_data.has_check_back_date)
184	
185	    if has_check_back and forecasts >= 2 and rule_phrases >= 1:
186	        score = 1.00
187	    elif (has_check_back and forecasts >= 1) or forecasts >= 2:
188	        score = 0.85
189	    elif has_check_back or forecasts >= 1:
190	        score = 0.65
191	    elif rule_phrases >= 1:
192	        score = 0.40
193	    else:
194	        score = 0.00
195	
196	    return SubScore(
197	        score=score,
198	        evidence={
199	            "rule": "progressivity_v1",
200	            "forecast_count": forecasts,
201	            "has_check_back_date": has_check_back,
202	            "decision_rule_phrases": rule_phrases,
203	        },
204	    )
205	
206	
207	def score_severity(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
208	    failure_modes = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "failure_modes")
209	    dissent = max(0, int(input_data.dissent_claim_count))
210	
211	    floor = min(1.0, 0.15 * len(failure_modes) + 0.10 * dissent)
212	
213	    judged = judge.judge(
214	        criterion="severity",
215	        prompt={
216	            "conclusion": input_data.conclusion_text,
217	            "failure_modes": failure_modes,
218	            "assumptions": _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "assumptions"),
219	            "dissent_claim_count": dissent,
220	        },
221	    )
222	    llm_score = _clamp01(judged.get("score", 0.0))
223	    score = max(floor, llm_score)
224	
225	    capped = False
226	    if not failure_modes and dissent == 0:
227	        score = min(score, 0.35)
228	        capped = True
229	
230	    track_record_ceiling = input_data.severity_track_record_ceiling
231	    track_record_capped = False
232	    if track_record_ceiling is not None:
233	        ceiling = _clamp01(track_record_ceiling)
234	        if score > ceiling:
235	            score = ceiling
236	            track_record_capped = True
237	
238	    # Drift penalty: multiplicative on the score, applied AFTER the
239	    # ceiling so that a method whose track record was already capped
240	    # gets penalized further still when it is actively drifting.
241	    drift_penalty = _clamp01(float(input_data.severity_drift_penalty))
242	    if drift_penalty < 1.0:
243	        score = score * drift_penalty
244	
245	    return SubScore(
246	        score=score,
247	        evidence={
248	            "rule": "severity_v1",
249	            "deterministic_floor": round(floor, 4),
250	            "llm_score": round(llm_score, 4),
251	            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
252	            "no_failure_modes_cap": capped,
253	            "track_record_ceiling": (
254	                None if track_record_ceiling is None else round(track_record_ceiling, 4)
255	            ),
256	            "track_record_capped": track_record_capped,
257	            "drift_penalty": round(drift_penalty, 4),
258	            "drift_penalty_applied": drift_penalty < 1.0,
259	        },
260	    )
261	
262	
263	def _topic_in_targets(topic_hint: str, targets: list[str]) -> bool:
264	    if not topic_hint:
265	        return True
266	    needle = topic_hint.strip().lower()
267	    if not needle:
268	        return True
269	    root = re.split(r"\W+", needle)[0]
270	    if not root:
271	        return True
272	    return any(root in (t or "").lower() for t in targets)
273	
274	
275	def score_aim_method_fit(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
276	    transfer_targets = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "transfer_targets")
277	    pattern_types = [p.pattern_type for p in input_data.profiles]
278	
279	    judged = judge.judge(
280	        criterion="aim_method_fit",
281	        prompt={
282	            "conclusion": input_data.conclusion_text,
283	            "topic_hint": input_data.topic_hint,
284	            "transfer_targets": transfer_targets,
285	            "pattern_types": pattern_types,
286	        },
287	    )
288	    score = _clamp01(judged.get("score", 0.0))
289	
290	    penalty = 0.0
291	    if input_data.topic_hint and not _topic_in_targets(
292	        input_data.topic_hint, transfer_targets
293	    ):
294	        penalty = 0.10
295	        score = max(0.0, score - penalty)
296	
297	    return SubScore(
298	        score=score,
299	        evidence={
300	            "rule": "aim_method_fit_v1",
301	            "llm_score": round(_clamp01(judged.get("score", 0.0)), 4),
302	            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
303	            "topic_hint": input_data.topic_hint,
304	            "transfer_targets": transfer_targets,
305	            "topic_outside_targets_penalty": penalty,
306	        },
307	    )
308	
309	
310	def score_compressibility(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
311	    assumptions = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "assumptions")
312	    n = len(assumptions)
313	
314	    judged = judge.judge(
315	        criterion="compressibility",
316	        prompt={
317	            "assumptions": assumptions,
318	            "reasoning_moves": _aggregate_profile_lists(
319	                input_data.profiles, "reasoning_moves"
320	            ),
321	        },
322	    )
323	    decorative = int(judged.get("decorative_count", 0) or 0)
324	    decorative = max(0, min(decorative, n))
325	    effective_n = max(1, n - decorative)
326	
327	    base = 1.0 / (1.0 + max(0, effective_n - 1) * 0.25)
328	    score = _clamp01(base)
329	
330	    return SubScore(
331	        score=score,
332	        evidence={
333	            "rule": "compressibility_v1",
334	            "assumption_count": n,
335	            "decorative_count": decorative,
336	            "effective_n": effective_n,
337	            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
338	        },
339	    )
340	
341	
342	def score_domain_sensitivity(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
343	    failure_modes = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "failure_modes")
344	    transfer_targets = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "transfer_targets")
345	
346	    judged = judge.judge(
347	        criterion="domain_sensitivity",
348	        prompt={
349	            "conclusion": input_data.conclusion_text,
350	            "topic_hint": input_data.topic_hint,
351	            "failure_modes": failure_modes,
352	            "transfer_targets": transfer_targets,
353	        },
354	    )
355	    raw = judged.get("score", None)
356	    if raw is None:
357	        # Backfill default: uncertain, not failed.
358	        score = 0.5
359	        used_default = True
360	    else:
361	        score = _clamp01(raw)
362	        used_default = False
363	
364	    floor = 0.10 if not failure_modes else 0.0
365	    score = max(score, floor)
366	
367	    return SubScore(
368	        score=score,
369	        evidence={
370	            "rule": "domain_sensitivity_v1",
371	            "llm_score": None if used_default else round(_clamp01(raw), 4),
372	            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
373	            "no_failure_modes_floor": floor,
374	            "used_uncertain_default": used_default,
375	        },
376	    )
377	
378	
379	def composite_score(
380	    *,
381	    progressivity: float,
382	    severity: float,
383	    aim_method_fit: float,
384	    compressibility: float,
385	    domain_sensitivity: float,
386	) -> float:
387	    mean_other = (
388	        SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["progressivity"] * _clamp01(progressivity)
389	        + SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["severity"] * _clamp01(severity)
390	        + SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["aim_method_fit"] * _clamp01(aim_method_fit)
391	        + SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["compressibility"] * _clamp01(compressibility)
392	    )
393	    return _clamp01(_clamp01(domain_sensitivity) * mean_other)
394	
395	
396	def score_conclusion(
397	    input_data: MqsInput,
398	    *,
399	    judge: Optional[MqsJudge] = None,
400	    model_name: str = "stub",
401	    prompt_version: str = PROMPT_VERSION,
402	) -> MethodologyQualityScore:
403	    """Produce a complete MQS for one conclusion. Composable with the peer-
404	    review swarm: callers may inject a real LLM judge or pass `judge=None` to
405	    use the deterministic stub (used by the backfill CLI when no LLM is
406	    configured)."""
407	    judge_ = judge or StubMqsJudge()
408	
409	    prog = score_progressivity(input_data)
410	    sev = score_severity(input_data, judge_)
411	    fit = score_aim_method_fit(input_data, judge_)
412	    comp = score_compressibility(input_data, judge_)
413	    dom = score_domain_sensitivity(input_data, judge_)
414	
415	    composite = composite_score(
416	        progressivity=prog.score,
417	        severity=sev.score,
418	        aim_method_fit=fit.score,
419	        compressibility=comp.score,
420	        domain_sensitivity=dom.score,
421	    )
422	
423	    return MethodologyQualityScore(
424	        conclusion_id=input_data.conclusion_id,
425	        progressivity=prog,
426	        severity=sev,
427	        aim_method_fit=fit,
428	        compressibility=comp,
429	        domain_sensitivity=dom,
430	        composite=composite,
431	        model_name=model_name,
432	        prompt_version=prompt_version,
433	    )
434	
435	
436	def evidence_payload(score: MethodologyQualityScore) -> dict[str, Any]:
437	    """Build the JSON blob persisted in `MethodologyQualityScore.evidence`.
438	
439	    Stable shape so a human reviewer can contest a sub-score and so the doc
440	    consistency check can verify keys."""
441	    return {
442	        "schema": MQS_SCHEMA,
443	        "composite_formula": COMPOSITE_FORMULA,
444	        "subscore_weights": SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS,
445	        "progressivity": score.progressivity.evidence,
446	        "severity": score.severity.evidence,
447	        "aim_method_fit": score.aim_method_fit.evidence,
448	        "compressibility": score.compressibility.evidence,
449	        "domain_sensitivity": score.domain_sensitivity.evidence,
450	    }
451	
452	
453	def evidence_payload_json(score: MethodologyQualityScore) -> str:
454	    return json.dumps(evidence_payload(score), sort_keys=True, default=str)
455	
456	
457	__all__ = [
458	    "COMPOSITE_FORMULA",
459	    "MQS_SCHEMA",
460	    "PROMPT_VERSION",
461	    "SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS",
462	    "MethodologyProfileSummary",
463	    "MethodologyQualityScore",
464	    "MqsInput",
465	    "MqsJudge",
466	    "StubMqsJudge",
467	    "SubScore",
468	    "composite_score",
469	    "evidence_payload",
470	    "evidence_payload_json",
471	    "score_aim_method_fit",
472	    "score_compressibility",
473	    "score_conclusion",
474	    "score_domain_sensitivity",
475	    "score_progressivity",
476	    "score_severity",
477	]
478

file.content:
"""Methodology Quality Score (MQS): operational form of the five working
criteria from THE_META_METHOD.md.

Each Conclusion that has at least one MethodologyProfile gets an MQS row with
five sub-scores in [0,1], an LLM-or-stub-judged evidence blob per sub-score,
and a composite that uses Domain Sensitivity as a multiplicative gate.

The composite formula and sub-score rubrics are documented in
docs/methods/MQS_Specification.md. The doc and this module are kept in sync
by scripts/check_mqs_doc_consistency.py.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import re
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any, Iterable, Optional, Protocol

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator


MQS_SCHEMA = "theseus.mqs.v1"
PROMPT_VERSION = "mqs-prompt-v1.0"

# Mirrored verbatim by docs/methods/MQS_Specification.md and verified by
# scripts/check_mqs_doc_consistency.py.
COMPOSITE_FORMULA = (
    "domain_sensitivity * mean(progressivity, severity, aim_method_fit, compressibility)"
)

SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS: dict[str, float] = {
    "progressivity": 0.25,
    "severity": 0.25,
    "aim_method_fit": 0.25,
    "compressibility": 0.25,
}

_DECISION_RULE_RE = re.compile(
    r"\b(if [a-z]+.*?then |we will |we'll |exit if |by [0-9]{4}|by q[1-4] |"
    r"check back |trigger |would falsif|will revisit)",
    re.IGNORECASE,
)
_EVIDENCE_STR_CAP = 600


class MqsJudge(Protocol):
    """LLM-judge contract. Tests pass a stub; production wires the real LLM."""

    def judge(self, *, criterion: str, prompt: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Return a dict with at least {"score": float in [0,1], "rationale": str}."""
        ...


@dataclass
class StubMqsJudge:
    """Deterministic judge used by tests and by the no-LLM backfill path.

    `responses` lets a test pin a per-criterion score; otherwise every call
    returns `default_score` with rationale "stub".
    """

    responses: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] | None = None
    default_score: float = 0.5

    def judge(self, *, criterion: str, prompt: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
        if self.responses and criterion in self.responses:
            return self.responses[criterion]
        return {"score": self.default_score, "rationale": "stub"}


class MethodologyProfileSummary(BaseModel):
    """Slimmed-down view of MethodologyProfile fields the scorer reads."""

    pattern_type: str = ""
    title: str = ""
    summary: str = ""
    reasoning_moves: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    transfer_targets: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    assumptions: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    failure_modes: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    confidence: float = 0.5


class MqsInput(BaseModel):
    """Everything the scorer needs about a conclusion to produce an MQS."""

    conclusion_id: str
    conclusion_text: str = ""
    rationale: str = ""
    topic_hint: str = ""
    profiles: list[MethodologyProfileSummary] = Field(default_factory=list)
    forecast_count: int = 0
    has_check_back_date: bool = False
    dissent_claim_count: int = 0

    # Track-record coupling (prompt 02). Caller looks up the relevant
    # `MethodTrackRecord` row(s) for this conclusion's domain via
    # `noosphere.evaluation.method_track_record.severity_ceiling_for` and
    # passes the resulting cap here. None means "no track record available
    # / no cap"; a value caps `score_severity` from above so that methods
    # with thin or poor track records cannot project high severity onto
    # new conclusions in their domain.
    severity_track_record_ceiling: Optional[float] = None

    # Drift coupling (prompt 04 + composition prompt). Multiplicative
    # penalty in (0, 1] applied to the Severity sub-score AFTER the
    # track-record ceiling. Callers derive this from the *inherited*
    # drift state across the method's composition DAG via
    # `noosphere.methods.composition.severity_penalty_multiplier_with_inheritance`,
    # NOT from the leaf state alone — a method whose composed dependency
    # is drifting must inherit that risk. 1.0 means "no penalty"
    # (default). Documented in docs/methods/MQS_Specification.md §
    # Drift coupling.
    severity_drift_penalty: float = 1.0


class SubScore(BaseModel):
    score: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    evidence: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)

    @field_validator("evidence")
    @classmethod
    def _cap_strings(cls, value: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return _cap_evidence(value)


class MethodologyQualityScore(BaseModel):
    """Operational form of the five meta-method criteria for one conclusion."""

    schema_: str = Field(default=MQS_SCHEMA, alias="schema")
    conclusion_id: str
    progressivity: SubScore
    severity: SubScore
    aim_method_fit: SubScore
    compressibility: SubScore
    domain_sensitivity: SubScore
    composite: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
    composite_formula: str = COMPOSITE_FORMULA
    model_name: str = "stub"
    prompt_version: str = PROMPT_VERSION
    scored_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))

    model_config = {"populate_by_name": True}


def _cap_evidence(value: Any) -> Any:
    if isinstance(value, str):
        return value[:_EVIDENCE_STR_CAP]
    if isinstance(value, list):
        return [_cap_evidence(item) for item in value]
    if isinstance(value, dict):
        return {k: _cap_evidence(v) for k, v in value.items()}
    return value


def _clamp01(x: float) -> float:
    if x != x:  # NaN
        return 0.0
    return max(0.0, min(1.0, float(x)))


def _decision_rule_phrase_count(text: str) -> int:
    return len(_DECISION_RULE_RE.findall(text or ""))


def _aggregate_profile_lists(
    profiles: Iterable[MethodologyProfileSummary],
    field: str,
) -> list[str]:
    out: list[str] = []
    for p in profiles:
        out.extend(getattr(p, field, []) or [])
    return out


def score_progressivity(input_data: MqsInput) -> SubScore:
    """Deterministic. Reads forecast count, check-back, decision-rule phrases."""
    text = f"{input_data.conclusion_text} {input_data.rationale}"
    rule_phrases = _decision_rule_phrase_count(text)
    forecasts = max(0, int(input_data.forecast_count))
    has_check_back = bool(input_data.has_check_back_date)

    if has_check_back and forecasts >= 2 and rule_phrases >= 1:
        score = 1.00
    elif (has_check_back and forecasts >= 1) or forecasts >= 2:
        score = 0.85
    elif has_check_back or forecasts >= 1:
        score = 0.65
    elif rule_phrases >= 1:
        score = 0.40
    else:
        score = 0.00

    return SubScore(
        score=score,
        evidence={
            "rule": "progressivity_v1",
            "forecast_count": forecasts,
            "has_check_back_date": has_check_back,
            "decision_rule_phrases": rule_phrases,
        },
    )


def score_severity(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
    failure_modes = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "failure_modes")
    dissent = max(0, int(input_data.dissent_claim_count))

    floor = min(1.0, 0.15 * len(failure_modes) + 0.10 * dissent)

    judged = judge.judge(
        criterion="severity",
        prompt={
            "conclusion": input_data.conclusion_text,
            "failure_modes": failure_modes,
            "assumptions": _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "assumptions"),
            "dissent_claim_count": dissent,
        },
    )
    llm_score = _clamp01(judged.get("score", 0.0))
    score = max(floor, llm_score)

    capped = False
    if not failure_modes and dissent == 0:
        score = min(score, 0.35)
        capped = True

    track_record_ceiling = input_data.severity_track_record_ceiling
    track_record_capped = False
    if track_record_ceiling is not None:
        ceiling = _clamp01(track_record_ceiling)
        if score > ceiling:
            score = ceiling
            track_record_capped = True

    # Drift penalty: multiplicative on the score, applied AFTER the
    # ceiling so that a method whose track record was already capped
    # gets penalized further still when it is actively drifting.
    drift_penalty = _clamp01(float(input_data.severity_drift_penalty))
    if drift_penalty < 1.0:
        score = score * drift_penalty

    return SubScore(
        score=score,
        evidence={
            "rule": "severity_v1",
            "deterministic_floor": round(floor, 4),
            "llm_score": round(llm_score, 4),
            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
            "no_failure_modes_cap": capped,
            "track_record_ceiling": (
                None if track_record_ceiling is None else round(track_record_ceiling, 4)
            ),
            "track_record_capped": track_record_capped,
            "drift_penalty": round(drift_penalty, 4),
            "drift_penalty_applied": drift_penalty < 1.0,
        },
    )


def _topic_in_targets(topic_hint: str, targets: list[str]) -> bool:
    if not topic_hint:
        return True
    needle = topic_hint.strip().lower()
    if not needle:
        return True
    root = re.split(r"\W+", needle)[0]
    if not root:
        return True
    return any(root in (t or "").lower() for t in targets)


def score_aim_method_fit(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
    transfer_targets = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "transfer_targets")
    pattern_types = [p.pattern_type for p in input_data.profiles]

    judged = judge.judge(
        criterion="aim_method_fit",
        prompt={
            "conclusion": input_data.conclusion_text,
            "topic_hint": input_data.topic_hint,
            "transfer_targets": transfer_targets,
            "pattern_types": pattern_types,
        },
    )
    score = _clamp01(judged.get("score", 0.0))

    penalty = 0.0
    if input_data.topic_hint and not _topic_in_targets(
        input_data.topic_hint, transfer_targets
    ):
        penalty = 0.10
        score = max(0.0, score - penalty)

    return SubScore(
        score=score,
        evidence={
            "rule": "aim_method_fit_v1",
            "llm_score": round(_clamp01(judged.get("score", 0.0)), 4),
            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
            "topic_hint": input_data.topic_hint,
            "transfer_targets": transfer_targets,
            "topic_outside_targets_penalty": penalty,
        },
    )


def score_compressibility(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
    assumptions = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "assumptions")
    n = len(assumptions)

    judged = judge.judge(
        criterion="compressibility",
        prompt={
            "assumptions": assumptions,
            "reasoning_moves": _aggregate_profile_lists(
                input_data.profiles, "reasoning_moves"
            ),
        },
    )
    decorative = int(judged.get("decorative_count", 0) or 0)
    decorative = max(0, min(decorative, n))
    effective_n = max(1, n - decorative)

    base = 1.0 / (1.0 + max(0, effective_n - 1) * 0.25)
    score = _clamp01(base)

    return SubScore(
        score=score,
        evidence={
            "rule": "compressibility_v1",
            "assumption_count": n,
            "decorative_count": decorative,
            "effective_n": effective_n,
            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
        },
    )


def score_domain_sensitivity(input_data: MqsInput, judge: MqsJudge) -> SubScore:
    failure_modes = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "failure_modes")
    transfer_targets = _aggregate_profile_lists(input_data.profiles, "transfer_targets")

    judged = judge.judge(
        criterion="domain_sensitivity",
        prompt={
            "conclusion": input_data.conclusion_text,
            "topic_hint": input_data.topic_hint,
            "failure_modes": failure_modes,
            "transfer_targets": transfer_targets,
        },
    )
    raw = judged.get("score", None)
    if raw is None:
        # Backfill default: uncertain, not failed.
        score = 0.5
        used_default = True
    else:
        score = _clamp01(raw)
        used_default = False

    floor = 0.10 if not failure_modes else 0.0
    score = max(score, floor)

    return SubScore(
        score=score,
        evidence={
            "rule": "domain_sensitivity_v1",
            "llm_score": None if used_default else round(_clamp01(raw), 4),
            "llm_rationale": str(judged.get("rationale", "")),
            "no_failure_modes_floor": floor,
            "used_uncertain_default": used_default,
        },
    )


def composite_score(
    *,
    progressivity: float,
    severity: float,
    aim_method_fit: float,
    compressibility: float,
    domain_sensitivity: float,
) -> float:
    mean_other = (
        SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["progressivity"] * _clamp01(progressivity)
        + SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["severity"] * _clamp01(severity)
        + SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["aim_method_fit"] * _clamp01(aim_method_fit)
        + SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS["compressibility"] * _clamp01(compressibility)
    )
    return _clamp01(_clamp01(domain_sensitivity) * mean_other)


def score_conclusion(
    input_data: MqsInput,
    *,
    judge: Optional[MqsJudge] = None,
    model_name: str = "stub",
    prompt_version: str = PROMPT_VERSION,
) -> MethodologyQualityScore:
    """Produce a complete MQS for one conclusion. Composable with the peer-
    review swarm: callers may inject a real LLM judge or pass `judge=None` to
    use the deterministic stub (used by the backfill CLI when no LLM is
    configured)."""
    judge_ = judge or StubMqsJudge()

    prog = score_progressivity(input_data)
    sev = score_severity(input_data, judge_)
    fit = score_aim_method_fit(input_data, judge_)
    comp = score_compressibility(input_data, judge_)
    dom = score_domain_sensitivity(input_data, judge_)

    composite = composite_score(
        progressivity=prog.score,
        severity=sev.score,
        aim_method_fit=fit.score,
        compressibility=comp.score,
        domain_sensitivity=dom.score,
    )

    return MethodologyQualityScore(
        conclusion_id=input_data.conclusion_id,
        progressivity=prog,
        severity=sev,
        aim_method_fit=fit,
        compressibility=comp,
        domain_sensitivity=dom,
        composite=composite,
        model_name=model_name,
        prompt_version=prompt_version,
    )


def evidence_payload(score: MethodologyQualityScore) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Build the JSON blob persisted in `MethodologyQualityScore.evidence`.

    Stable shape so a human reviewer can contest a sub-score and so the doc
    consistency check can verify keys."""
    return {
        "schema": MQS_SCHEMA,
        "composite_formula": COMPOSITE_FORMULA,
        "subscore_weights": SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS,
        "progressivity": score.progressivity.evidence,
        "severity": score.severity.evidence,
        "aim_method_fit": score.aim_method_fit.evidence,
        "compressibility": score.compressibility.evidence,
        "domain_sensitivity": score.domain_sensitivity.evidence,
    }


def evidence_payload_json(score: MethodologyQualityScore) -> str:
    return json.dumps(evidence_payload(score), sort_keys=True, default=str)


__all__ = [
    "COMPOSITE_FORMULA",
    "MQS_SCHEMA",
    "PROMPT_VERSION",
    "SUBSCORE_WEIGHTS",
    "MethodologyProfileSummary",
    "MethodologyQualityScore",
    "MqsInput",
    "MqsJudge",
    "StubMqsJudge",
    "SubScore",
    "composite_score",
    "evidence_payload",
    "evidence_payload_json",
    "score_aim_method_fit",
    "score_compressibility",
    "score_conclusion",
    "score_domain_sensitivity",
    "score_progressivity",
    "score_severity",
]
